gloss.en: in a different way

words.example: Boys and girls may behave differently.

gloss.en: a very large animal that lived long ago

words.example: The children love the big dinosaur.

gloss.en: the way a person or thing moves or points

words.example: They walked in the direction of the village.

gloss.en: a senior manager who runs a company

words.example: She is a director at the company.

gloss.en: to have a different opinion

words.example: Even friends disagree sometimes.

gloss.en: to stop being seen

words.example: The sun can disappear behind the clouds.

gloss.en: a place or party for dancing to music

words.example: We danced all night at the disco.

gloss.en: to find or learn something for the first time

words.example: They wanted to discover something new.

gloss.en: a conversation about something

words.example: We had a long discussion about the plan.

gloss.en: feeling that things are spinning around you

words.example: I felt dizzy after the long ride.

gloss.en: finished; completed

words.example: My homework is done.

gloss.en: a play for theatre, TV or radio

words.example: We watched a crime drama on TV.

gloss.en: to make a picture with a pen or pencil

words.example: She can draw beautifully.

gloss.en: a box-like part of furniture that slides out

words.example: I put my socks in the drawer.

gloss.en: a picture made with a pen or pencil

words.example: She made a pencil drawing of the cat.

gloss.en: images and feelings in your mind while asleep

words.example: I had a really weird dream last night.

gloss.en: a tool for making holes

words.example: He used a drill to make a hole.

gloss.en: a journey in a car or vehicle

words.example: Let's go for a drive.

gloss.en: a medicine, or an illegal substance

words.example: He does not smoke or take drugs.

gloss.en: a shop that sells medicines and other goods

words.example: I bought soap at the drugstore.

gloss.en: not wet; without water

words.example: Is my shirt dry yet?

gloss.en: a bird that swims and has webbed feet

words.example: A duck is swimming on the lake.

gloss.en: all through a period of time

words.example: She slept during the long flight.

gloss.en: near the beginning of a period of time

words.example: I woke up in the early morning.

gloss.en: to get money for work you do

words.example: He earns money by teaching.

gloss.en: a piece of jewellery worn on the ear

words.example: She wore one gold earring.

gloss.en: the planet that we live on

words.example: We all live on the earth.

gloss.en: without problems or difficulty

words.example: You can find the place easily.

gloss.en: in or towards the east

words.example: They live on the east coast.

gloss.en: the process of teaching and learning

words.example: A good education is very important.

gloss.en: a change caused by something; a result

words.example: Exercise has a good effect on health.

gloss.en: also (used after negatives)

words.example: Pete can't go and I can't either.

gloss.en: using or producing electricity

words.example: They bought an electric car.

gloss.en: a very large grey animal with a long trunk

words.example: We saw herds of elephants.

gloss.en: a machine that carries people between floors

words.example: We took the elevator to the top floor.

gloss.en: a message sent between computers

words.example: I sent you an email this morning.

gloss.en: in danger of no longer existing

words.example: The panda is an endangered animal.

gloss.en: the last part of a story or film

words.example: The story has a happy ending.

gloss.en: full of energy; very active

words.example: She is an energetic young woman.

gloss.en: a person who designs or builds machines or roads

words.example: My brother is an engineer.

gloss.en: extremely large

words.example: They live in an enormous house.

gloss.en: as much as is needed

words.example: I did not train enough for the game.

gloss.en: to come or go into a place

words.example: Knock before you enter.

gloss.en: the wish to have what someone else has

words.example: Her new bike filled me with envy.

gloss.en: a mistake

words.example: I found a spelling error in the text.

gloss.en: a moving staircase

words.example: We rode the escalator to the next floor.

gloss.en: more than usual; in particular

words.example: I love fruit, especially apples.

gloss.en: a short piece of writing on a topic

words.example: I have to write an essay this weekend.

gloss.en: the money used in some European countries

words.example: I paid five euros for it.

gloss.en: at any time

words.example: Nothing ever happens here.

gloss.en: happening every day; ordinary

words.example: This is part of everyday life.

gloss.en: in or to every place

words.example: I have looked everywhere.

gloss.en: facts or signs that show something is true

words.example: There is clear evidence of a problem.

gloss.en: in every way; precisely

words.example: It's exactly nine o'clock.

gloss.en: a test of what you know or can do

words.example: I have to take an exam next week.

gloss.en: extremely good

words.example: This is an excellent book.

gloss.en: physical activity to stay healthy

words.example: Swimming is good exercise.

gloss.en: to be real or present

words.example: Does life exist on other planets?

gloss.en: to think that something will happen

words.example: We expect good news soon.

gloss.en: to make something easy to understand

words.example: Can you explain the difference to me?

gloss.en: a reason that tells why something happened

words.example: He gave a simple explanation for the delay.

gloss.en: to show a feeling or opinion in words

words.example: Teachers expressed concern about the changes.

gloss.en: a word or phrase

words.example: What is the meaning of this expression?

gloss.en: to a very high degree

words.example: It is extremely important to be careful.

gloss.en: a thing that is known to be true

words.example: Here are some basic facts about healthy eating.

gloss.en: a building where goods are made by machine

words.example: He works in a car factory.

gloss.en: to not succeed in doing something

words.example: Many diets fail because they are boring.

gloss.en: acceptable and right

words.example: They offered a fair price.

gloss.en: a person who admires or enjoys something

words.example: She is a big music fan.

gloss.en: extremely good; excellent

words.example: He's done a fantastic job.

gloss.en: land used for growing crops or keeping animals

words.example: They live on a large farm.

gloss.en: a popular style of clothes or hair

words.example: She follows the latest fashion trends.

gloss.en: weighing too much; having much flesh

words.example: The cat is getting very fat.

gloss.en: a kind act that helps someone

words.example: Can you do me a favor?

gloss.en: being a woman or a girl

words.example: She is a female student here.

gloss.en: a small number; some

words.example: We've had a few replies.

gloss.en: writing about imaginary people and events

words.example: She enjoys reading popular fiction.

gloss.en: an area of land for crops or animals

words.example: We walked across the green field.

gloss.en: a struggle using physical force

words.example: There was a fight in the street.

gloss.en: a person who fights

words.example: He is a brave fighter.

gloss.en: moving pictures with sound that tell a story

words.example: Let's stay in and watch a film.

gloss.en: happening at the end

words.example: This is his final day at work.

gloss.en: after a long time or delay

words.example: The show finally started late.

gloss.en: the last part or end of something

words.example: It was a close finish in the race.

gloss.en: before anyone or anything else

words.example: She won first prize.

gloss.en: an animal that lives and breathes in water

words.example: They caught several fish.

gloss.en: a person who catches fish

words.example: The fisherman sat by the river.

gloss.en: the activity of catching fish

words.example: They often go fishing.

gloss.en: a tiny jumping insect that bites animals

words.example: The dog has a flea.

gloss.en: a journey made by air in a plane

words.example: We had a smooth flight to Paris.

gloss.en: a small flying insect with two wings

words.example: A fly was buzzing against the window.

gloss.en: able to fly

words.example: Bees are flying insects.

gloss.en: thick cloud near the ground that is hard to see through

words.example: We could not drive in the thick fog.

gloss.en: a cover for holding papers or files

words.example: I keep my notes in this folder.

gloss.en: to come or go after someone

words.example: Follow me please.

gloss.en: a person who plays football

words.example: He wants to be a famous footballer.

gloss.en: intended to be given to or used by someone

words.example: There's a letter for you.

gloss.en: from a country that is not your own

words.example: What foreign languages do you speak?

gloss.en: a large area thickly covered with trees

words.example: We walked through a pine forest.

gloss.en: a tool with points used for eating food

words.example: We eat with a knife and fork.

gloss.en: by good luck

words.example: Fortunately, the meeting had not started.

gloss.en: towards a place in front

words.example: He took a step forwards.

gloss.en: honest and direct in what you say

words.example: To be frank, I did not like it.

gloss.en: recently made or picked; not old

words.example: Is this milk fresh?

gloss.en: a machine that keeps food cold and fresh

words.example: Put the milk in the fridge.

gloss.en: cooked in hot oil

words.example: I had fried eggs for breakfast.

gloss.en: a small animal that lives on land and in water

words.example: A frog jumped into the pond.

gloss.en: that you enjoy; enjoyable

words.example: She's really fun to be with.

gloss.en: items like tables, chairs and beds

words.example: We bought new furniture for the room.

gloss.en: a room or building for showing art

words.example: We saw the paintings at the gallery.

gloss.en: a strong-smelling plant used in cooking

words.example: She added garlic to the soup.

gloss.en: a substance like air, not solid or liquid

words.example: Air is a mixture of gases.

gloss.en: a door-like barrier in a fence or wall

words.example: Please close the front gate.

gloss.en: a thing you give to someone

words.example: She gave me a nice gift.

gloss.en: particular; already stated

words.example: At any given time, the shop is busy.

gloss.en: a net for scoring in some sports; an aim

words.example: He kicked the ball into the goal.

gloss.en: bright yellow in colour, like gold

words.example: The name was written in gold letters.

gloss.en: a game of hitting a small ball into holes

words.example: I play golf every weekend.

gloss.en: of high quality or an acceptable standard

words.example: This is a good book.

gloss.en: having a pleasant, attractive face
